channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Former Atlanta mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms mulling Georgia gubernatorial run
item.description: Former Atlanta mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms said she is considering running in Georgia's 2026 gubernatorial election. She last served in the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs order that likely sets in motion a future ban on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order taking aim at DEI programs in the military
item.description: Trump said the diversity programs discourage merit and leadership, and discriminate on the basis of race and sex. The directive was among a series of orders that seeks to reshape the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie, MPR News

item.title: News for Trump: Denmark's last envoy to Greenland says U.K. may have first dibs
item.description: Denmark's last minister for Greenland says Britain once claimed right of first refusal over the territory — because of its proximity to Canada, which Britain once ruled.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer, MPR News

item.title: Doctors opposing RFK Jr. rally in the lead-up to his confirmation
item.description: Many health professionals are lining up against Trump's pick for health secretary. They say his anti-vaccine views could cost lives. Some of his supporters embrace his stance.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: In a tighter Minnesota Legislature, coalitions pushing middle path could play big role
item.description: The conditions could be ripe for a rise of the Capitol middle, with close margins in both chambers requiring cooperation to move proposals forward.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: K-12 education and the Minnesota Legislature: 5 things to watch this session
item.description: Even as Minnesota lawmakers struggle to start their new session, education committee leaders in the House and Senate are quietly starting to set budget and policy priorities. Here’s a look at what might be coming.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Republicans vie to replace Matt Gaetz in crowded 10-way special election primary
item.description: Ten Republicans are vying to replace former Rep. Matt Gaetz in a crowded primary election kicking off on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is 'cool' online, and Democrats are scrambling to catch up
item.description: Many left-leaning creators actually agree that Trump is considered cool online, and influencers helped get him there.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fills his government with billionaires after running on a working-class message
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s brash populism has always involved incongruence: the billionaire businessman-politician stirring the passions of millions who, regardless of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kash Patel’s podcast persona: staunch Trump defender and fierce critic of the FBI he could soon lead
item.description: Kash Patel positioned himself as a steadfast Donald Trump loyalist well before the president picked him to run the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Ali Swenson And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In the early going, Trump 2.0 approach on foreign policy is to talk loudly and carry a big stick
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump in his first week back in the White House has offered an early preview to his second-term foreign policy approach: Talk loudly and wield a big stick.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida House and Senate leaders take charge in special session showdown
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis pushed for a special session to help boost President Donald Trump's immigration efforts. But that session was blocked by Senate and House leadership Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu hopes to meet Trump in Washington as soon as next week, US officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is hoping to meet with President Donald Trump in Washington as early as next week, according to two U.S. officials familiar with preliminary planning for the trip.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Comer launches investigation of sanctuary cities, asks mayors to testify
item.description: U.S. James Comer is asking sanctuary mayors to testify before the House Committee on Oversight and Reform on their sanctuary city policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pushes border crackdown at House Republican retreat as he caps first week in office
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump regaled House Republicans on Monday with a rhetorical highlight reel from his first week in office, and he urged them to support his immigration crackdown and border security proposals. “We have no apologies and we’re moving very fast," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, WILL WEISSERT and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favo
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favorable to the presi
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Spending showdown: Republicans will need to corral votes – but they haven't asked, yet
item.description: One of Washington's biggest untold stories right now is Congress' inaction on a "topline" spending number for the rest of the fiscal year – a possible sign of an impending time crunch.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tucker Carlson's son joins Vance's press office
item.description: Buckley Carlson previously worked as a top aide to Rep. Jim Banks (R-Ind.), the former chairman of the conservative Republican Study Committee and a Trump ally.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Health experts prep Dem lawmakers on anti-vaccine arguments ahead of RFK Jr's confirmation hearings
item.description: Public health experts answered questions from Democratic Senators about whether vaccines cause autism, whether manufacturers should be immune from liability for vaccine injuries, whether vaccines are adequately studied, and more, during a round table discussion about anti-vaccine "misinformatio
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Bessent as Trump's Treasury secretary
item.description: Scott Bessent on Monday secured confirmation to lead the Department of the Treasury, putting President Trump’s top economic nominee in place ahead of a daunting GOP push to enact Trump's ambitious economic agenda. Senators voted 68-29 on Bessent’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: A victory for Trump's 'FAFO': How the White House strong-armed one-time close ally Colombia over immigration
item.description: Colombia did an about-face at lightning-fast speed on accepting deportation flights in what President Donald Trump hailed as a victory for his "f--- around and find out" [FAFO] style of governing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by Indiana sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bishop who enraged Trump says she was speaking from faith
item.description: Trump, who sat alongside JD Vance, appeared annoyed, looking away from Budde as she spoke directly to him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: First lady Melania Trump’s new official portrait revealed
item.description: Melania Trump's new official White House portrait was released on Monday. The first lady is all business in the black and white picture, wearing a suit while striking a power pose.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Experts warn about the ’crumbling infrastructure’ of federal government data
item.description: Unstable federal funding puts at risk the government statistics used to track the U.S. economy and population, officials and data users warn. That’s before any cuts by President Trump and Congress.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents’ immigration status.
item.dc:creator: EUGENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis: North Florida could see several inches of snow
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis warned of potential snowfall this week, amid the freezing temperatures impacting northern Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gen. Milley portrait taken down from Pentagon
item.description: The portrait, which had just been unveiled Jan. 10, hung in the Joint Chiefs hallway next to those of other former chairs.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5
